title: The Sea Around Us
excerpt: A poetic and scientifically revelatory portrait of the world's oceans, from their primeval origins to the latest oceanographic discoveries.
keyIdeas: The ocean is the cradle of all life on Earth and remains the planet's dominant force
Ocean currents and tides act as Earth's great regulator of climate and temperature
The deep sea is a vast, largely unknown frontier teeming with life
Humanity's relationship with the sea is one of dependence, discovery, and responsibility
keyTakeaways: The ocean's physical systems — currents, tides, chemistry — govern planetary conditions
Life in the sea is interconnected in ways that surface observation cannot capture
Scientific understanding of the ocean was still in its infancy in the 1950s
The sea holds both the history of the planet and the key to its future
whoShouldRead: Ocean enthusiasts and marine science students
Anyone who loves poetic nature writing
Historians of environmental science and conservation
Readers interested in the origins of modern oceanography
whoShouldSkip: Those seeking a field guide or identification manual
Readers wanting up-to-date oceanographic data (the science has advanced)
